note: Both our features need core.py's Option class, so let's split it by region instead of by file to avoid clobbering. I only need: (1) tail of Option.__init__ (add ~10 lines validating dict-based dynamic defaults, after your batch_size init code - I'll add mine after whatever you add), (2) a new small Option.get_default override (Option class currently has no get_default override - net-new method), (3) a small elif branch inside existing Option.get_help_extra (~line 2870, next to the existing 'inspect.isfunction... (dynamic)' check), (4) Command.main() - add a second-pass resolution call before ctx.invoke(). I will NOT touch process_value, type_cast_value, or to_info_dict - those are yours. I don't need types.py at all (verified convert_type already falls back to STRING gracefully for non-standard default types via the guessed_type flag, so dict defaults don't break type inference). Go ahead and own types.py fully plus process_value/type_cast_value/to_info_dict/batch_size storage in __init__. Ping me before you touch get_help_extra or add a get_default override so we don't collide.
